entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop species Pennisetum americanum studied for its response to water stress

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The developmental stage of the plant during flowering

entities.name: PRE-FLOWERING
entities.description: The developmental stage of the plant before flowering

entities.name: STOMATAL RESPONSE
entities.description: The opening and closing behavior of stomata in response to water stress

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: The condition of drought or lack of water affecting the plant

entities.name: LEAF WATER POTENTIAL
entities.description: The measure of the energy state of water in the leaves

entities.name: OSMOTIC ADJUSTMENTS
entities.description: The process of maintaining turgor through solute accumulation

entities.name: TURGOR POTENTIAL
entities.description: The pressure exerted by the cell contents against the cell wall

entities.name: ABA
entities.description: Abscisic acid, a plant hormone that regulates stomatal closure

entities.name: LEAF CONDUCTANCE
entities.description: The rate of water vapor passage through the leaf stomata

entities.name: PANICLE DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The growth and emergence of the panicle in pearl millet

relationships.description: The study examines the stomatal response of pearl millet to water stress
Water stress induces the closure of stomata
Stomata remain more open during the flowering stage under water stress
Stomata close more readily during the pre-flowering stage under water stress
Stomatal closure occurs at specific leaf water potential thresholds
Stomatal closure is associated with the loss of turgor potential
Higher levels of ABA in pre-flowering plants are associated with stomatal closure
The presence of an emerged panicle is related to the tendency of stomata to remain open
Water stress leads to increased levels of ABA in pre-flowering plants
The relationship between leaf water potential and turgor potential was similar in flowering and pre-flowering plants
The flowering stage is characterized by the emergence of the panicle
